purpose: Add a dedicated Ananta CLI mode for admins who want to diagnose and repair local Windows 11 or Ubuntu systems in a controlled, reviewable way.
goal: Ananta can help admins diagnose installation and runtime problems on Windows 11 and Ubuntu, evaluate logs, propose corrective actions and optionally perform bounded repair steps with review and verification.
success_criteria: The CLI supports a dedicated system diagnosis and repair flow for Windows 11 and Ubuntu.
Logs and system context can be collected in a bounded and reviewable way.
The system proposes likely causes and concrete repair steps.
Repairs are never blindly applied without policy and confirmation.
Verification steps confirm whether the proposed repair actually improved the system state.

tasks.title: Use-case and mode definition add
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated admin repair CLI mode is documented as an official Ananta use case.
The mode clearly targets Windows 11 and Ubuntu local system diagnosis and repair.
The mode is positioned as review-first and not as unrestricted autonomous shell execution.

tasks.title: CLI entrypoint for admin repair add
acceptance_criteria: A clear CLI entry such as diagnose-system or repair-system exists.
The mode supports dry-run and bounded execution options.
The command is discoverable in help and docs.

tasks.title: Operating system detection and environment summary implement
acceptance_criteria: The CLI can detect whether it runs on Windows 11 or Ubuntu.
A summarized environment report includes OS, shell, package tools, services and runtime basics.
The environment summary is visible before repair planning.

tasks.title: Bounded system context collection define
acceptance_criteria: The mode defines exactly which system facts, logs and command outputs may be collected.
Collection stays bounded and visible to the user.
Sensitive data is redacted or explicitly excluded by default.

tasks.title: Log source adapters for Windows 11 implement
acceptance_criteria: The mode can collect useful Windows diagnosis sources such as event logs, service state, PATH/runtime checks and selected command outputs.
Collection failures degrade cleanly.
Collected sources are normalized into Ananta artifacts.

tasks.title: Log source adapters for Ubuntu implement
acceptance_criteria: The mode can collect useful Ubuntu diagnosis sources such as journalctl, systemd service state, apt/dpkg health and selected command outputs.
Collection failures degrade cleanly.
Collected sources are normalized into Ananta artifacts.

tasks.title: Problem class taxonomy define
acceptance_criteria: Common admin problem classes are defined for Windows and Ubuntu.
At minimum, package/runtime, PATH, permissions, service, port/conflict and Docker/WSL-like classes are covered.
The taxonomy supports diagnosis, routing and repair planning.

tasks.title: Diagnosis report artifact implement
acceptance_criteria: The mode produces a structured diagnosis artifact.
The artifact includes likely causes, supporting evidence, confidence and next steps.
The artifact is reviewable and storable like other Ananta results.

tasks.title: Repair plan generation add
acceptance_criteria: The mode can generate a bounded repair plan from diagnosis results.
The repair plan contains ordered steps, risks, expected effects and verification hints.
The plan distinguishes safe suggestions from high-risk actions.

tasks.title: Repair action policy define
acceptance_criteria: Repair actions are categorized into allowed, confirm-required and blocked classes.
Dangerous system mutations are never run implicitly.
The policy integrates with existing governance and audit ideas.

tasks.title: Dry-run mode implement
acceptance_criteria: All repair plans can be previewed without applying changes.
The user can see the exact proposed commands or actions.
Dry-run is the default or strongly recommended first path.

tasks.title: Step-by-step confirmed execution mode add
acceptance_criteria: Repairs can be executed one step at a time with explicit confirmation.
The system logs which actions were accepted, skipped or blocked.
Users can stop the sequence without corrupting state.

tasks.title: Verification phase after repair implement
acceptance_criteria: After repair steps, the system re-checks relevant health signals.
The result clearly states whether the issue appears resolved, improved or still broken.
Verification output is attached to the same diagnosis/repair record.

tasks.title: Windows-specific repair playbooks add
acceptance_criteria: There are initial playbooks for common Windows 11 issues.
At minimum, runtime/path, service and Docker/WSL-style issues are covered.
Playbooks remain bounded and reviewable.

tasks.title: Ubuntu-specific repair playbooks add
acceptance_criteria: There are initial playbooks for common Ubuntu issues.
At minimum, apt/dpkg, systemd service and runtime/toolchain problems are covered.
Playbooks remain bounded and reviewable.

tasks.title: Admin-facing output formatting improve
acceptance_criteria: CLI output is readable and structured for real repair work.
Diagnosis, plan, risks and verification are visually separated.
The output avoids dumping raw logs without explanation.

tasks.title: Audit trail for repair sessions add
acceptance_criteria: Repair sessions are auditable.
The system records gathered evidence, proposed actions, accepted actions and verification results.
Sensitive data is handled safely.

tasks.title: Rollback and caution model define
acceptance_criteria: The system distinguishes reversible from non-reversible actions.
Plans explicitly warn when rollback is limited or unavailable.
High-risk steps surface stronger caution language and confirmation requirements.

tasks.title: Golden path for Windows repair document
acceptance_criteria: There is one official Windows 11 repair demo flow.
The flow covers context collection, diagnosis, plan, confirmation and verification.
Another developer can reproduce it.

tasks.title: Golden path for Ubuntu repair document
acceptance_criteria: There is one official Ubuntu repair demo flow.
The flow covers context collection, diagnosis, plan, confirmation and verification.
Another developer can reproduce it.

tasks.title: Contract and smoke tests for repair mode add
acceptance_criteria: The diagnosis and plan output formats are protected by tests.
At least one smoke path exists for Windows and one for Ubuntu using bounded fixtures or mocks.
The tests verify that execution remains confirmation-gated.

tasks.title: Future extension path for broader admin domains isolate
acceptance_criteria: Later expansions such as network repair, container repair or app-specific repair are documented separately.
The first release remains focused on core Windows 11 and Ubuntu installation/runtime repair scenarios.
The roadmap does not bloat the MVP.
